description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged and angular form, featuring a mix of rough and slightly smoother surfaces. The rock is predominantly covered in rusty orange and brown hues, with patches of white mineral deposits visible on several faces. One side displays a distinct white area with the number '47' written in black, indicating human marking or cataloging. The surface texture appears coarse and weathered, with some areas showing more pronounced pitting and natural erosion. The coloration suggests the presence of iron oxide or similar minerals, giving it a vibrant, earthy appearance. This rock would likely be found as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park, roadside, or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, orange-brown rock with white mineral patches and a visible human-made marking, featuring a coarse, weathered surface.
description_view_0: This view shows a broad, sloped face with a large white mineral patch and mottled orange-brown coloration.
description_view_1: This side highlights the white area with the number '47' written on it, surrounded by rusty orange textures.
description_view_2: The rock appears darker and more uniformly orange-brown, with fewer visible white patches and a more angular profile.
description_view_3: This angle reveals a mix of white mineralization and orange-brown surfaces, with a prominent ridge and rough texture.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped roadside area
